summary_text: **Read-Only Production Verification Checklist (`x-auto`)** Use only read paths (`GET`, file reads, process/status reads). Do not run publish/sync/patch commands. 1. **Scheduler health** - Scheduler process is running (single active writer, no duplicate scheduler processes). - Heartbeat/log timestamp is fresh (within expected cycle window). - No stuck posting lock (`Status=posting` + stale `Locked By` token). - Next due slot logic is alive (no due `approved` row left unprocessed beyond tolerance). 2. **Queue ↔ Notion consistency** - For active rows (`draft/approved/failed/duplicate_blocked`), `id/notion_page_id/status/scheduled_for/title` are consistent between local queue and Notion. - No row is `posted` in Notion while still present as active in queue. - No unintended local drift markers remain (stale dirty metadata repeatedly reappearing). - Failed rows are explainable by current validation rules (not unexplained state flips). 3. **X API auth/read path** - Read-only auth check passes (`get_me`/equivalent identity read). - Recent posted tweet IDs in Notion/logs are fetchable via API (not hard-failed by auth scope/rate-limit issues). - Rate-limit state is healthy enough for scheduler window (no immediate exhaustion). 4. **Logs and runtime evidence** - Recent scheduler logs have no unhandled exceptions, retry storms, or repeated lock failures. - `logs/posted.json` appends are monotonic and contain expected `tweet_id/posted_at/notion_page_id`. - Failure counts in recent window are within acceptable threshold (or explicitly explained incidents). 5. **Repost / citation regression checks** - For manually deleted/reposted cases, latest canonical tweet ID is the one in Notion (`Tweet ID`/`Posted At`). - Old reply artifacts from prior attempts are not lingering when they should be removed. - `Source URLs` / `Reply URLs` are context-fit for body claims (or intentionally blank). - No unsupported quantified outcome claims remain without traceable primary source mapping. **Claim “production runtime is operating normally” only if all 5 sections pass.**
